name: Cubs Game Day
description: Go to a Cubs game at Wrigley Field. Soak in the ivy-covered outfield walls, sing along to 'Go Cubs Go,' and grab a Chicago-style hot dog while you're at it.

name: Bottomless Brunch at Batter & Berries
description: Do bottomless brunch at Batter & Berries in Lincoln Park. Enjoy bottomless mimosas (or bottomless water if you're keeping it chill) and try their famous lemon dreamsicle french toast.

name: Bean There, Done That
description: Visit Cloud Gate (aka The Bean) in Millennium Park. Get the classic reflection photo but also find a creative angle that makes it look surreal or distorted.

name: River Selfie Stop
description: Take a selfie next to the Chicago River from the Riverwalk. Bonus points if you catch the green-dyed river on St. Patrick's Day or a water taxi in the background.

name: Deep Dish Showdown
description: Try deep dish pizza from Lou Malnati's or Giordano's and decide which side of the Chicago pizza debate you're on. Document the cheese pull.

name: Skydeck Ledge Step
description: Step out onto the glass Ledge at Willis Tower's Skydeck on the 103rd floor. Look straight down and try to keep your cool for a full 30 seconds.

name: Architecture Boat Tour
description: Take a Chicago Architecture Foundation river cruise and learn about the buildings lining the river. Pick your favorite building and explain why.

name: Navy Pier Ferris Wheel Ride
description: Ride the Centennial Wheel at Navy Pier and snap a photo of the skyline from the top. Time it for sunset if you can.

name: Lincoln Park Zoo Wander
description: Visit Lincoln Park Zoo (it's free!) and find your spirit animal. Spend at least 30 minutes and snap a photo of your favorite exhibit.

name: Wicker Park Bar Hop
description: Visit three bars in Wicker Park in one night, each with a different vibe. Try a craft cocktail at one, a dive beer at another, and a mezcal at the third.

name: Italian Beef Baptism
description: Order an Italian beef sandwich dipped and with hot giardiniera from Portillo's or Al's #1 Italian Beef. Eat it standing up like a true Chicagoan.

name: Art Institute Masterpiece Hunt
description: Visit the Art Institute of Chicago and find three famous works: American Gothic, A Sunday on La Grande Jatte, and Nighthawks. Take a selfie with your favorite.

name: Lakefront Trail Run or Ride
description: Walk, run, or bike at least two miles of the Lakefront Trail along Lake Michigan. Take in the skyline views and stop for a breather at one of the beaches.

name: Hidden Speakeasy Discovery
description: Find the hidden entrance to The Violet Hour in Wicker Park. There's no sign — just a mysterious unmarked door. Order their signature cocktail.

name: Pilsen Mural Walk
description: Walk through Pilsen's 16th Street and find at least three colorful murals. Take a creative pose next to your favorite one.

name: Garrett Popcorn Mix
description: Grab the famous Chicago Mix (caramel and cheese popcorn together) from Garrett Popcorn Shops on Michigan Ave. Decide once and for all if sweet and savory belong together.

name: Comedy Show Night
description: Catch an improv or sketch comedy show at Second City or iO Theater. Laugh until your cheeks hurt and try not to get picked for audience participation.

name: Magnificent Mile Window Shopping
description: Walk the full stretch of the Magnificent Mile on Michigan Avenue. Pop into three stores and find one item you'd realistically buy.

name: Chinatown Dim Sum Run
description: Visit Chicago's Chinatown and try dim sum at a traditional spot. Order at least three dishes you've never tried before.

name: Buckingham Fountain Photo
description: Capture Buckingham Fountain in Grant Park with the skyline behind it. Visit at night for the light show if possible.

name: Jazz Club Night
description: Visit a live jazz club like Green Mill or Andy's Jazz Club and stay for at least two full sets. Chicago jazz is legendary — soak it in.

name: Chicago-Style Hot Dog
description: Order a proper Chicago-style hot dog with the full seven toppings. No ketchup — that's the rule. Try Superdawg or Portillo's for the authentic experience.

name: Museum of Science & Industry Explore
description: Visit the Museum of Science and Industry in Hyde Park. Find the U-505 submarine exhibit and learn one surprising fact about its capture.

name: Rooftop Bar Skyline Toast
description: Visit a rooftop bar like Cindy's at the Chicago Athletic Association and toast to the skyline view over Millennium Park.

name: 360 Chicago Tilt
description: Go to 360 Chicago at the former John Hancock Center and do the TILT experience — lean out over Michigan Ave from the 94th floor.

name: Vintage Thrift in Wicker Park
description: Dig through the vintage and thrift shops along Milwaukee Ave in Wicker Park. Find one piece that tells a story.

name: Chicago Riverwalk Stroll
description: Walk the full length of the Chicago Riverwalk from Lake Shore Drive to Lake Street. Stop at one of the bars or cafés along the way.

name: Latte Art Snap
description: Order a latte with art at a local Chicago café like Intelligentsia or Metric Coffee. Capture a close-up before your first sip.

name: Field Museum Discovery
description: Visit the Field Museum and say hi to Sue the T. Rex. Explore at least two exhibit halls and learn one fact that surprises you.

name: Street Busker Tip
description: Stop and listen to a street performer on Michigan Ave or in an L station for at least one full song and tip them.

name: Neon Sign Night Capture
description: Find a classic neon sign in Chicago at night — try the Green Mill, a theater marquee, or a dive bar sign — and take a moody photo.

name: Boba Break in Chinatown
description: Visit a boba tea shop in Chinatown and try a flavor you've never had before. Go for something adventurous like taro or brown sugar.

name: Yoga in the Park
description: Attend or self-lead a 15-minute yoga flow in Grant Park or Lincoln Park. Capture proof with the skyline in the background.

name: Logan Square Coffee Crawl
description: Try coffee from two different cafés in Logan Square and compare tasting notes like a pro. Gaslight Coffee Roasters is a great start.

name: Maxwell Street Market Find
description: Visit the Maxwell Street Market on a Sunday morning. Try the famous Maxwell Street Polish and find one unexpected treasure.

name: Adler Planetarium Skyline View
description: Walk out to the Adler Planetarium peninsula for the most iconic Chicago skyline photo op. Capture the full skyline with the lake in frame.

name: Food Truck Chase
description: Track down a Chicago food truck and order their specialty item. Share a quick review of what you got.

name: Blues Club Experience
description: Visit a legendary Chicago blues club like Buddy Guy's Legends or Kingston Mines. Stay for at least one full set and feel the soul of the city.

name: Hidden Bookstore Find
description: Visit a local independent bookstore like Myopic Books or Open Books and find a book by a Chicago author.

name: North Avenue Beach Chill
description: Spend at least 30 minutes at North Avenue Beach. Dip your toes in Lake Michigan if the weather allows and take in the skyline view.

name: Local Distillery Tasting
description: Take a tour or do a tasting at a local distillery like Koval or CH Distillery. Learn how their signature spirit is made.

name: L Train Photo
description: Capture the iconic Chicago L train rumbling through the Loop from street level. Try to get the train framed between the buildings.

name: Sunrise at Montrose Harbor
description: Catch the sunrise at Montrose Harbor overlooking Lake Michigan. Spend five minutes just reflecting in silence.
